wiki_value: Bank Identification Number. First 6 or 8 digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank and card product. Values < 10,000,000 are 6-digit BINs; >= 10,000,000 are 8-digit BINs. Clustered index key for fast lookups. (Tier 1 - Dictionary.CountryBin6 upstream wiki)
truth_value: Bank Identification Number (6-digit). First 6 digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank and card product. Part of the composite primary key with CountryID. Source upstream table enforces BinCode < 10,000,000 via CHECK constraint; 8-digit BINs are stored separately in Dictionary.CountryBin8.
contradicting_fact_verbatim: The CHECK constraint (BinCode < 10000000) ensures only 6-digit BINs are stored here. BINs of 8 digits or more belong in Dictionary.CountryBin8.

wiki_value: Sample covariance between price changes of instruments A and B. Formula: `SUM(a*b)/N - SUM(a)*SUM(b)/N²`. Positive = instruments move together; negative = opposite directions. (Tier 2 - SP_Dim_Instrument_Correlation_FilterByInstrumentID)
truth_value: Population covariance between hourly price changes of instruments A and B. Formula: SUM(a*b)/N - SUM(a)*SUM(b)/N². Divides by N (not N-1), making it population covariance. Positive = instruments move together; negative = opposite directions.
contradicting_fact_verbatim: cast(sum(PriceChange_a*PriceChange_b) as decimal(38,20))/count(*)-(cast(sum(PriceChange_a)*sum(PriceChange_b) as decimal(38,20)))/power(count(*),2.00) as Covariance
raw_context: Sample covariance between price changes of instruments A and B. Formula: `SUM(a*b)/N - SUM(a)*SUM(b)/N²`. Positive = instruments move together; negative = opposite directions. (Tier 2 - SP_Dim_Instrument_Correlation_FilterByInstrumentID)

wiki_value: Pearson correlation coefficient: `Covariance / (StdDev_a * StdDev_b)`. Range: -1.0 (perfect inverse) to +1.0 (perfect correlation). NULL when either StdDev is zero (flat price). (Tier 2 - SP_Dim_Instrument_Correlation_FilterByInstrumentID)
truth_value: Pearson correlation coefficient: Covariance / (StdDev_a * StdDev_b). Range: -1.0 to +1.0. Rows where either StdDev is zero are excluded by the HAVING clause, so this column is never NULL for that reason.
raw_context: Pearson correlation coefficient: `Covariance / (StdDev_a * StdDev_b)`. Range: -1.0 (perfect inverse) to +1.0 (perfect correlation). NULL when either StdDev is zero (flat price). (Tier 2 - SP_Dim_Instrument_Correlation_FilterByInstrumentID)

wiki_value: Primary key identifying the payment state. 1=New, 2=Approved, 3=Decline, 4=Technical, 5=InProcess, 6=Canceled, 7=Confirmed. (Tier 1 — Dictionary.PaymentStatus)
truth_value: Primary key identifying the payment state. 1=Pending, 2=InProcess, 3=Processed, 4=Canceled, 5=Failed, 6=Reversed, 7=CompletedExternally. (Tier 1 — Dictionary.PaymentStatus)
contradicting_fact_verbatim:
1 | Pending | Payment initiated but not yet entered processing. Initial state for all new payment records. User may see "processing" in their deposit history. |
| 2 | InProcess | Payment is actively being processed by the billing engine or payment provider. Funds have not yet been credited. |
| 3 | Processed | Successfully completed — funds have been credited to the user's account. Terminal success state. Balance is updated. |
| 4 | Canceled | Payment was canceled before completion. No funds moved. Terminal failure state. |
| 5 | Failed | Payment processing encountered an error. No funds moved. Terminal failure state. User may retry. |
| 6 | Reversed | A previously processed payment was reversed. Common causes: chargeback, duplicate detection, manual correction. Debits the credited amount from the user's account. |
| 7 | CompletedExternally | Payment was processed and reconciled outside the normal automated pipeline.
raw_context: Primary key identifying the payment state. 1=New, 2=Approved, 3=Decline, 4=Technical, 5=InProcess, 6=Canceled, 7=Confirmed. (Tier 1 — Dictionary.PaymentStatus)

wiki_value: Whether user can cancel the redeem at this stage. True=cancelable, False=positions are closing or closed (point of no return). False: PositionClosed(6), TransactionInProcess(7), TransactionDone(8), Terminated(20). (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, Dictionary.RedeemStatus)
truth_value: Whether the user can cancel the redeem request at this stage. 1=cancelable, 0=committed. Per upstream wiki, only Pending (ID 1) is cancelable; InProcess, Completed, Failed, and CompletedPartially are all IsCancelable=0. The False list must include upstream IDs 2, 3, 5, 6 at minimum, not just IDs 6, 7, 8, 20.
contradicting_fact_verbatim: 1=cancellable (Pending), 0=committed (InProcess, Completed, Failed). The cancel boundary is the point when positions start closing.
